description: Diagnostic contract for the generated negative corpus. Each fixture is a deliberately malformed, license-clean .vrm authored by tools/generate_negative.py that provokes exactly one importer diagnostic. tests/test_usdvrm_negative.py drives the freshly built importer over each and pins the code + mechanism. Regenerate the binaries with `python plugins/usdVrmFileFormat/tools/generate_negative.py`.

mechanisms.read-fatal: The container is unreadable, so Usd.Stage.Open fails and the importer surfaces the code in the raised Tf error (never on a stage).
mechanisms.import-warning: The stage opens; the importer records the coded warning on /Asset.customData.vrm:warnings, and it is the only diagnostic the fixture emits.

fixtures.note: GLB JSON chunk's opening brace is corrupted; cgltf rejects the container.
material.baseColorTexture.index points past a non-existent textures array; cgltf index validation rejects it.
A node translation serializes NaN/Infinity tokens, which cgltf's JSON parser rejects.
VRMC_vrm value is a JSON array, not an object; cgltf requires object-valued extensions and rejects the container. (This is why the ERROR-level VRM002 is unreachable through a cgltf-validated file.)
JOINTS_0 references local joint 5 in a 2-joint skin; the influence is clamped to the skeleton root.
Humanoid bone 'spine' references node 999, which does not exist; the bone is skipped.
A VRM 0.x humanBones array maps 'hips' twice; the first mapping is kept and the rest ignored.
An expression morphTargetBind names index 7 on a mesh with one morph target; the bind is skipped.
A SpringBone spring references collider-group index 5 when only one group exists; the reference is dropped.
